rag_rules: AGREE contracts must be placed in valid scopes: component type annexes for assumptions/guarantees, implementation annexes for bindings.
Implementation-level bindings should use assign only with existing output features or declared auxiliary variables.
Do not reference implementation-only subcomponent paths in component type annexes.
generic_rules: AGREE guarantees should relate visible outputs to visible inputs in type scope.
Use only visible AADL features or declared helpers in AGREE expressions.
Generated AGREE code must not introduce unresolved identifiers or invalid types.
Component type contracts may not reference implementation-specific paths.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Constants and equations in AGREE must use valid scalar types: int, real, bool.
Assignments in implementation scope must target existing output features.
anti_patterns: Do not place guarantee statements in implementation scope; use type scope for output dependency contracts.
Avoid referencing subcomponent ports or implementation paths in component type annexes.
Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers not present in the model.
irrelevant_topics: AADL package references and with clauses
AGREE validator diagnostics and repair strategies
Model fusion and owner/scope preservation rules
